entry.title: Re: CSRF validation fails (403 Forbidden) from SAP Build Work Zone approuter
entry.content:
Hello @smarchesini, here is the content of the DetailsExt.controller.js file where the FileUploader behaviour is implemented. As noted earlier, handling CSRF token works without issues when running without the CF's approuter.

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/m/MessageBox"
], function (MessageBox) {
    "use strict";
    return  {

        onInit: function (oEvent) {

        },

        onFileChange: function (oEvent) {
            var oFileUploader = oEvent.getSource();
            var oFile = oFileUploader.oFileUpload.files[0];
            this._file = oFile;
            console.log("File selected:", oFile);

            /* Destroy the header before each new uplaod.
               Otherwise the parameters are just accumulating on each consecutive call
               and the CSRF tokens are not recognised by SAP Server and refused */
            oFileUploader.destroyHeaderParameters();

            var headerParmaSlug = new sap.ui.unified.FileUploaderParameter();
            headerParmaSlug.setName('slug');
            headerParmaSlug.setValue(oFileUploader.getValue());
            oFileUploader.addHeaderParameter(headerParmaSlug);

            /* Get the CSRF token value and add as new header parameter */           
            this.csrfToken = this.getView().getModel().getSecurityToken();
            var headerParmaCSRF = new sap.ui.unified.FileUploaderParameter();
            headerParmaCSRF.setName('x-csrf-token');
            headerParmaCSRF.setValue(this.csrfToken);
            oFileUploader.addHeaderParameter(headerParmaCSRF);

        },
        afterDialogClose (oEvent) {
            var oParameters = oEvent.getParameters()

        },
        onUploadStart:function () {

        },       
        onUploadPress: function () {
/*
        Not used, because FileUploader is set up to start uploading immediately 
        after choosing a file and the button is hidden 
*/
        },
        onUploadComplete: function (oEvent) {
            var sResponse = oEvent.getParameter("response");
            var sResponseRaw = oEvent.getParameter("responseRaw");
            var sStatus = oEvent.getParameter("status");
            if (sResponse) {
                MessageBox.show("Upload complete: " + sResponse);
                var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
                oModel.refresh(true);
            } else {
                MessageBox.show("Upload failed." + sResponseRaw );
            }
        }
    }
});

entry.title: Key Updates on Validating a Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment with Rule Functions
entry.content:
You must define rules to validate the employment information, job information, and compensation information for a higher duty or temporary assignment. If these rule functions are not configured, there could be data inconsistencies for the higher duty or temporary assignment, and the higher duty or temporary assignment may not work as intended in the standard solution.

 

Introduction

When you create or edit a higher duty or temporary assignment, you would have defined rules to validate the employment information, job information, and compensation information. In the rule definition for the corresponding rule functions, you can define the validations to be carried out and the validation messages that must be displayed when the validations fail.

If you did not configure these rule functions, there could be data inconsistencies for the higher duty or temporary assignment.

With the higher duty or temporary assignment moving to Centralized Services in Employee Central, these validations occur automatically, whether or not you've configured the rules.

If you had configured the rules, then you only need to disable the rules, to prevent the automatic validations from the centralized services from raising duplicate validation messages. 

However, if you did not configure these rule functions, these validations were not taking place for your higher duty or temporary assignment. With the move to centralized services, these validations will run always whenever you create or edit a higher duty or temporary assignment.

If the higher duty or temporary assignments you created contained inconsistent data -- such as having a higher duty whose duration is outside the boundary of the corresponding nominal assignment -- then these validations from centralized services will alert you of such inconsistent data, and your solution may not work as intended.

Therefore, we recommend that you configure the validation rules for your higher duty or temporary assignment. For information about the different use cases covered by these validations and how to configure these validation rules, refer to the following:

  • Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Employment Information
    • Define Rule to Validate Employment Information

  • Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Job Information

    •  Define Rule to Validate Job Information.

  • Validate Higher Duty/Temporary Assignment Compensation Information
    • Define Rule to Validate Compensation Information

 

 

Summary

To summarize, we saw the importance of defining rules to validate the employment information, job information, and compensation information for a higher duty ore temporary assignment. In the rule definition for the corresponding rule functions, you can define the validations to be carried out and the validation messages that must be displayed when the validations fail.

Configuring these rule functions is very important because these validations will happen in the background when higher duty or temporary assignment runs on centralized services and may trigger unexpected warnings. For more information about centralized services in Employee Central, refer to Centralized Services in Employee Central.

For more details about implementing higher duty or temporary assignment, check out the Implementing Higher Duty or Temporary Assignment guide.

Thank you for reading. I request you to share your feedback by leaving a comment.

entry.title: Allocation in SAC
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a scenario that I need help with. I upload data with a split on the members of [d/COUNTRY] and on [d/STORE] = "#". The same total is uploaded afterwords, but the other way around i.e. with a split on the members of [d/STORE] and [d/COUNTRY = "#". I now need to merge these two together with a Data Action while having almost the same original split on [d/COUNTRY] and [d/STORE].

Anyone have any ideas?

entry.title: deletion of validation can´t be transported with fiori app F4406 Manage Substitutions a. Validations
entry.content:
Dear experts,

we have created a validation rule in the Fiori App F4406 "Manage Substitutions and Validations for Financial Documents". (S/4 HANA 2023 private Cloud)
In testsystem, we found some issues with the validation and it needs to be deleted.

We´ve deleted the validation in development, but there was not prompt to save the deletion to a transport.

Transporting with t.code GTC9 doesn´t work, in SAP GUI this validations are not even displayed.

Does anyone know what how to transport the deletion?

thanks,

entry.title: Re: Transport from onpremise sap systems to RISE with SAP
entry.content:
Hi,

Please find below answers to your queries-

1. can we do client export on-premise and import to S/4HANA cloud,private edition? - Please refer to roles and responsibility matrix document "SAP Client Operations" section.
2. Can we get the transfer request on-premise and import it into S/4HANA cloud,private edition?- Please refer to roles and responsibility matrix document  "Transport Management section".
3. If there is a version difference between on-premise (2021) and S/4HANA cloud,private edition (2023), is it acceptable to import the transfer in unconditional mode? - Please refer to roles and responsibility matrix document  "Transport Management section".

Regards,

Shilpi

entry.title: Israel: PDI BADI for Exclusion of Withholding Tax Items
entry.content:
In this blog, you learn how to exclude invoices, including payment and clearing documents, using Enhancement Implementation in SAP Cloud Applications Studio. This customization helps you exclude invoices that are not required for tax reporting in SAP Business ByDesign.

You need both the payment and clearing documents for tax reporting. You must decide whether to send both types of documents. In Israel, there are two types of business scenarios.

1. Scenario that requires only payment documents reported to the tax authority.

2. Scenario that requires both payment and clearing documents reported.

The current enablement of this feature meets the need of all Israel customers. To address this, PDI BADI offers an option that caters to both types. If you require only Payment documents, you can delete all Clearing documents and use only Payment documents for Tax Reporting. If you need both Payment and Clearing documents, you can use both for Tax Reporting.

To meet this requirement, the customer needs to implement an additional logic at the backend with the help of your respective partners. This logic deletes unwanted documents that are not considered for Tax Reporting. The step-by-step procedure below explains on how to implement the additional (Deletion) Logic.

1. In this example, two supplier invoices are created for a customer.

2. Two supplier charges are created against each of the two supplier invoices created in Step 1.

3. Perform “Manual Clearing” for the supplier charges against the corresponding supplier invoices.

A Partially Cleared line item appears as shown below.

4. Perform “Pay Manually by” Outgoing Bank Transfer to initiate the payment of 67 ILS.

We do a posting for the same.

Repeat step 3 and 4 to perform one more round of Manual Clearing and Pay Manually by” Outgoing Bank Transfer for the balance payment.

5. Initiate Withholding Tax (WHT) 856 Return by creating WHT Return Run.

In this, two records with UUID’s 2582B9AAB8041EDF8DA4BD12BD533023 (UUID for clearing document) and 2582B9AAB8041EDF8DA5223DC6433026 (UUID for the payment document) are passed. As per the logic in BAdI, all the Split items (clearing and the payment documents) are passed to the customer.

Let's assume that out of these two split items, you deleted one split item (clearing document) based on the newly implemented logic and report the other split item (payment document). Consider that you have deleted 2582B9AAB8041EDF8DA4BD12BD533023 and sent 2582B9AAB8041EDF8DA5223DC6433026. As per logic the system will consider only the record received from you for Tax Reporting purpose.

Below is the screenshot for the Tax Return ID generated.

If you check the Tax Return Tab, you can see that we had 2 records to be reported but due to customer/partner enhancement only one record was finally reported for the Tax Reporting purpose.

Export Parameters

CALL BADI lb_pdi_wht_return->delete_split_items

EXPORTING

is_input_data = ls_nodeid_tab

CHANGING

cs_result_data = lt_result.

LS_NODEID_TAB is the export parameter with all node IDs.

Customer will receive node IDs through the highlighted parameter.

Please find below the sample code:

Find below the sample ABSL code, which is placed in the Cloud Application Studio, tested with one UUID number and successfully deleted the same. 

Below are the technical details of the development and cloud application. 

S.No

Technical Name

Description

1

Namespace

https://sap.com/xi/AP/DueItemManagement/Global

2

Enhancement Option

DeleteWHTSplitItems

3

Enhancement Option Type

Multiple Use

4

Business Object

WithholdingTaxDeclaration

5

Structure

glo_il_uuid

6

Enhancement Spot

ES_GLO_PDI_IL_WHT_RETURN

7

BADI Definition

PDI_BADI_IL_WHT_RETURN

8

Interface

IF_BADI_IL_WHT_RETURN

9

Method

DELETE_SPLIT_ITEMS

The PDI BAdI handles both the below Israel scenarios for Withholding Tax Returns (856, 857, 102).

· Scenarios that require only Payment documents

· Scenarios that require Payment documents as well as Clearing documents.

For customers intend to use both payment and clearing documents for Tax Reporting purposes, the existing functionality will work as earlier and need not have to perform any new action.

entry.title: Report for Credit Risk Based impairment // SAP CLOUD PUBLIC EDITION
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am currently in the Explore Phase of a SAP Cloud PE Project. The customer wants to valuate the open customer positions. Due to some features the advanced valuation has, we want to go with it instead of the classic valuation. 

Now we want to valuate the "Doubtful account valuation". For the valuation you use the further valuations App in the classic valuation. For Advanced valuation you use the credit risk based impairment (GL- JOB). The Revaluation of the Customer positions does work in the Job log. Now we want to analyze the valuation and the "allowance" per debitor. In the Classic valuation this is possible with the Doublful Account Valuation APP. Here you can see the open Positions per Customer on a ovedue- interval grid and the accumulated allowance as well as the percentage (allowance/OP). 

Sadly the "Allowance" Field is connected to the results of the "further valuations" App and the there used  valuation method 3. So my allowance, calculated in the GL Job is not displayed here: 

I definetly need a report that shows me the Open Positions of a customer per overdue- interval grid as well as the accumulated allowance amount per customer (Idealy also per document). Is there any Standard way of getting the same report with the advanced valuation as I can get with the classic valuation? 

If not, can I copy the Doubtful Accounts Valuation App and extend the report with a value field of the accumulated allowance amount? 

 

Otherwise, is it recommendable to use the classic valuation approach instead? We have no problem with that, but heard, that it might run out of SAP Support in the near/middle future? 

 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: IAS to SAC: User Updates Not Provisioned
entry.content:
Some user properties are mapped as SAML attributes by default. When a property is mapped, it can not be updated via the SCIM API, which will include user provisioning services. Simply unmap these properties. Go to SAP Analytics Cloud, as the system owner, Menu-Security-Users, and select the button to 'Map SAML attributes'. This opens a dialogue box allowing you to select which attributes to unmap (or remap if you've previously removed the mapping). The first name and last name are mapped attributes, as well as others. If you want these to be updated via the SCIM API rather than via SAML then you'll need to unmap them.

If this isn't your issue (or even if it is), you may find the sample Postman Collections helpful in validating whether the issue is at the SAP Analytics Cloud end or with your provisioning mapping service. The user guide also provides a little more detail on the mapped attributes mentioned above.

entry.title: Activate Generic Object Services for PR
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 

I have a case where for PR in ME52N and ME53N, the Generic Objects Services are disabled. The 'Relationships' button is greyed out. 

 

Could anyone please let me know how to activate this ? Can this be achieved by using the class 'CL_GOS_MANAGER'. If Yes, any suggestions on how to do it ?

 

Help Greatly appreciated. Best Regards.

entry.title: Re: Currency conversion in People Story reports
entry.content:
Hi @CSmith,

thank you very much for quick reply!

I've checked the lookup table and I have all currencies to GBP only. Nothing from GBP to other currencies. Table is from 2023 so some slight differences in currency rates can appear. 

Lookup table:

Story Report:

Currency Converter:

Is it possible to check why the system probably is using the inverse of the rate? I think, it should use data from lookup table.

I need in my Story for VRP currencies to GBP not the other way around.

Regards,

Aneta

entry.title: Re: TRM Treasury - Letter of Credit – Configuration

entry.title: Re: Vendor with Cust(XVERR) Field is not visible in UI in...
entry.content:
Hi , 

I was also facing this problem and then I found the SNOTE 3265032 from SAP where they have explained that with the code changes in note 2469063 it is a standard logic that field XVERR will be hidden in UI.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003265032

Further they have explained that as a workaround, after adding the corresponding field of "Clearing with Customer" or "Clearing with Vendor" in the corresponding UIBB, you should also add the corresponding LIFNR and KUNNR in the general data section of ERP Customer and ERP Vendor UIBB and then the function will work.

I have tested this and it works absolutely fine for us.

 

 

BR 

Ajaya Kumar Mishra

entry.title: How to fill item navigation property of standard API to create Supplier Invoice in S4 HANA CLoud
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am working in S4 HANA public cloud and my requirement is to create Supplier Invoice by passing header data and navigation property "to_SelectedServiceEntrySheets" of API "/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV/A_SupplierInvoice".

I have created service consumption model for this API in eclipse. But, in the CREATE operation of consumption model no code generated to navigate to "to_SelectedServiceEntrySheets" as I need to supply two fields here "ServiceEntrysheet" and ServiceEntryItem" as this is mandatory to POST supplier invoice.

I have successfully supplied the header data and when I execute the code, I am getting message "No Item data supplied to post invoice". This is because above navigation property is not supplied in the payload.

Kindly suggest how I can supply the data into "to_SelectedServiceEntrySheets" of this API.

I have attached my code for reference.

Many Thanks In advance!

entry.title: Re: Deep Dive: Execution order item and Billable maintenance order
entry.content:
One question, it is possible to have an account assignment on this billable maintenance order and to post this acc ass (for example an internal order) to the service order with advanced execution ?
Thanks Michaela

entry.title: How to post Deferred Expenses without Purchase Order // prepaid expenses and deferred charges
entry.content:
Hi, 

I am using a SAP Cloud Public Edition System. I am receiving Invoices from Vendors (no Purchas Order) that are for future Periods, but due today. I want to pay the invoices today and also pay the Input Tax today. As far as I understood the Standard Process is as follows: 

1. Poste the Vendor Invoice against a Deferral Account

2. Create a recurring journal document that Debits the expense account and credits the deferral account. 

Like this I have a Invoice due today, the tax is reported today, but the expense is deferred to another period. The problem with this is, that I do have to be aware of 2 seperat steps. The Invoices are received via Ariba and are transfered in the system automatically. I dont want anyone checking all the invoices, if they have to be deferred. Isnt there a one step process, where I can set the Period/Periods in which the expense actually accures in the invoice? 

Thanks in advance!

we have this requirement: when a Request for Quotation (RFQ) is created the email output via BRF+ should be sended to the email address of the SAP user which created the RFQ. 

How can we configure this requirement?

Thank you in advance

entry.title: IBP Format sheet - Can we do directly in IBP Production system or Alternate options ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts, All,

IBP Format sheet - Can we do directly in IBP Production system or let me know Alternate options ?

 

As per user requirement, we have made 'Conditional Formatting' (If Key Figure Cell Value is NOT Equal to '0' (Zero) then display in Red color for the IBP Key Figure.

This color code Formatting is done in 'IBPFormattingSheet' for the Key Figure in 'IBP DEVELOPMENT System' and tested and working fine.

 

The Question is :

Do we need to do this IBPFormatting cell Color Code Formatting directly in Planning View Templates in IBP PRODUCTION system ?

Or is there any way/option to do in IBP Development system and do Transport or Export from Development system to Quality system and to Production system ?

 

Appreciate your help in this regards.

Thanks.

Parthi

entry.title: Call for ideas/volunteers/community drivers
entry.content:
Hello All,

How can we make our SAP Community in Georgia more active and open for discussions?

If you feel that you have some information to share, or may be some idea what can be done, please, do not hesitate to share in this group. If you would like to become a community driver, also, please, show up here.

I understand that everyone has very tight schedule, but let's share our thoughts, plans and achievements here.

We also need to think about SAP activities for the next year. It would be great if we organize SAP Inside Track in Tbilisi. Just to have an idea of this event, here are some Inside Tracks, held in 2024 in other countries:

SAP Inside Track Warsaw 2024 – Key Highlights, Ins... - SAP Community

Recap of SAP CodeJam and SAP Inside Track Vilnius ... - SAP Community

SAP Inside Track the Netherlands - #sitNL 2024 (ma... - SAP Community

So, let's speak up. Any news or idea from anyone is equally interesting - someone attended an interesting SAP event, someone has passed the critical project milestone, has interesting info, or solved a difficult case related to SAP project, anything...

Let's start talking to each other 🙂

entry.title: Re: Unable to Use {*} and {**} Operators in APIRule v2alp...
entry.content:
Dear Abhinav,

Make sure that you are using the latest release of the API Gateway module. In case you are using BTP Kyma runtime, the release that includes support for those operators (2.9.0) did not reach regular channel yet at this time.

entry.title: Delegation for Performance Reviews 2H 2024 Release Feature Target Population
entry.content:
Dear SAP community, 

We are having some issues with setting up the target population to maximize the PMGM Delegation Feature efficiently. 

Ideally, the permissions should be set up as below:

Delegation Management Permission (delegators)

e.g. Admin -> Managers

Delegator-Delegatee Relationship -> (delegatees)
e.g. Managers - Managers

Delegator-Employee Relationship (Subject User of the form to be delegated)
e.g. HR Admin - all
e.g. Managers - Direct Reports

For the first two permission roles wherein the target population is Managers, the question is how can we group all of them together as usually they cannot be grouped using a people pool filter as they are random managers assigned to employees. How are we able to go about this? 

Please advise. 

Thanks and Regards,

Kat

Come and join the second SAP meetup in Thessaloniki!

If you need any kind of information drop us an email at sapthessaloniki@gmail.com

Reach out to me for more details.

Regards

Prasath

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between SOAP RM and SOAP 1. in...
entry.content:
Hi Upendra,

1. SOAP 1.x Adapter

Purpose:

Used for standard SOAP-based communication.

Supports SOAP 1.1 and SOAP 1.2 protocols.


Features:

Allows synchronous and asynchronous message exchange patterns.

Commonly used for Web Services Description Language (WSDL)-based web services.

Offers various authentication methods like HTTP Basic, OAuth, and Client Certificate.

Supports attachments (MIME, MTOM).

Typically used for lightweight, stateless communications.


Use Case:

When integrating with SOAP-based web services that do not require guaranteed delivery or message persistence.

For scenarios where the communication is synchronous, and the external system expects a real-time response.

---

2. SOAP RM Adapter (Reliable Messaging)

Purpose:

An extension of the SOAP protocol that ensures reliable delivery of messages.

Built on WS-ReliableMessaging (WS-RM) standards.


Features:

Guarantees message delivery even in case of communication failures (e.g., network issues).

Includes message persistence, retries, and acknowledgment.

Ensures ordered delivery and duplicates prevention (if configured).

Typically used for asynchronous communication where reliability is critical.


Use Case:

When you need guaranteed delivery of SOAP messages to the target system.

For scenarios requiring transactional integrity, such as financial transactions or critical data exchanges.

---

How to Choose Between SOAP RM and SOAP 1.x in SAP CPI ?

Use SOAP RM Adapter:

If the integration scenario requires reliable delivery, retries, and message persistence.

When interacting with systems supporting WS-RM and reliability is essential (e.g., SAP systems like S/4HANA).

Asynchronous scenarios where the sender system doesn't need immediate responses.


Use SOAP 1.x Adapter:

For standard SOAP-based communication with less complexity.

When the web service being called doesn’t support WS-ReliableMessaging.

For synchronous message exchanges or lightweight integrations where reliability isn't a concern.

---

Key Differences:

---

Configuration Perspective in SAP CPI

SOAP RM Adapter: Choose this in the adapter configuration when the target system supports WS-RM and you need reliability.

SOAP 1.x Adapter: Use this for generic SOAP services that don’t need WS-RM.

entry.title: Re: Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Journal Entries: A Guide to Business Contexts and Rules
entry.content:
Hi Ciara,

I cannot create any rule with 'Market Segment' Business Content or Event in on-premise implementation.

However, even the first example 'Set Profit Center', was created using 'Market Segment.'

How can we activate this event and be able to create a rule?

Our version is SAP S/4HANA 2023 and SAP FIORI FOR SAP AC 2.0

entry.title: Re: Activate Generic Object Services for PR
entry.content:
AFAIK the icon is not greyed if there are no attachment/relationships 

  • AFAIK it's only a display, check 1851646 - New GOS icon.
  • Are you writing on inbound or outbound IDOC?
  • You could check your distribution model (BD64) and partner profiles (WE20) for the relevant message type

entry.title: Re: SAP integration suite data in excel sheet
entry.content:
Hi Sohail,

Please use the iflow design as a starting point and to take design idea/ inspirations to achieve your requirements. If you directly use the iflow as is, it might not suffice.

entry.title: Re: WM Bin sort
entry.content:
Hello @sap_mm31 

You won't be able to define such a sorting in start because your stack level is defined with 2 or 3 chars, e.g. 00, 14, 100. 

One option is to change storage bin numbering so the stack level is always defined with 3 chars, i.e. 000, 014, 100. Then standard sorting will work.

Another option is to update LAGP-SORLP field with a custom report. This way you can define sorting which ever way you need.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Copy sequence not workinq from AO only RSPLAN trace mode, BPC 11.1 Embedded Model BW4/HANA
entry.content: Hello Christina. We are facing the same issue. Did you manage to solve this?

Regards

Prasath

entry.title: Re: Building side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP: Automate...

entry.title: SAC VDT
entry.content:
Here’s a rephrased version of your message:

Hello,

I’m encountering an issue in the SAC system with the VDT drill-into feature. After using drill-down, I’m unable to retain the original structure that I had before drilling in, and the hierarchy gets disconnected from the VDT.

I would like to know if there’s any option to preserve the initial structure.

Thanks in advance.

This version clarifies the issue and maintains a professional tone.

entry.title: Procurement report
entry.content:
Hello,

I'd like to create a report in Sap business by design showing the following data:

Product, on hand quantity (in stock), demand quantity (shows all the aggregate open production orders), ordered quantity (shows all the aggregate open purchase orders).

Which data sources can give the above results?

This report will use to make sure all demands are covered.

Regards

entry.title: Re: From FIORI LUNCHPAD, 500 Internet server errors occur...
entry.content:
@dahliahana 

Please check tr-cd: ST22. If you find a runtime error related to your action (opening a WDA tile), please search for the relevant SAP notes.

entry.title: Re: SAP ECC Purchase Order Creation Automatic
entry.content:
Hello @NikolaNikolov 

There are actually two questions in your post.

Do you think that it is possible to set up somehow the transformation of Purchase Requisitions into Purchase Orders, even if they require approval? I have been reading, but cannot find a way to do it.

Here the answer is no. It's not possible to create purchase orders against purchase requisitions that are not approved yet. If it were possible, it would defeat the whole purpose of purchase requisition approvals - purchasing process is not started unless a purchase requisition is not approved. 

 

If it is possible, once we have the Purchase Requisition approved, to send a notification to user X, based on Y criteria?

That can be implemented with purchase requisition approval workflow.

 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: CDS views that can be used to Distinguish fields chan...
entry.content: Hi Gahan, Yes, currently, I don't find C1-released CDS view which can be used as data source(can be used in Data Browser or build to Custom CDS view). The view C_SalesChangeDocItemDEX is for data extraction purpose, and it DCL0 by default(no data when select). can you try with privileged access? e.g. SELECT * FROM C_SalesChangeDocItemDEX WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_slsdocchangedocitem).

entry.title: BTP CodeJam from Mark9
entry.content:
Hello Community Members,

We from Mark9 are planning to host SAP BTP CodeJams in the following locations in Q1/Q2 2025. Please let me know (comment below) if you are interested and we will plan the sessions accordingly based on the number of interested participants

 

  • India
    1.  Cloud Application Programming (CAP)
    2. SAP Build
  • Singapore
    1. Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
    2. ABAP Cloud & RAP
  • Thailand
    1. SAP Build
  • UAE
    1. Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
    2. SAP Integration Suite
  • USA
    1. Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
    2. SAP Integration Suite
    3. SAP Build

 

 

Regards,

Giri

entry.title: Re: Form not shown in MyInbox Workzone standard
entry.content:
@CoenAmista 

I had a similar issue where a task UI was not loaded in the "My Inbox" app. My issue was resolved by turning off the "Asynchronous module loading" parameter of the site, as described in the SAP note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003527309

entry.title: Re: Setting Default Values for Create in ABAP RESTful app...
entry.content:
For anyone else landing in here, in hopes of finding the answer to how you can add initial/default values to your entity (f.ex. for hiding fields, or defaulting types, etc) - currently there's no way to achieve this on-prem, in an unmanaged scenario, in your behavior pool class - or in metadata on your cds views...

The only way I could find, to achieve this requirement - was to add a controller extension to your Fiori Elements app / object page, and adding something along the lines of the following snipplet of code to it:

 

        onInit: function() {
            this.extensionAPI.attachPageDataLoaded($.proxy(this.onPageLoaded, this)); 
        },

        onPageLoaded: function(oEvent){
            if(this.getView().getModel("ui").getProperty("/createMode")) {
                var ctx = this.getView().getBindingContext();
                ctx.getModel().setProperty(ctx.getPath() + "/<Property>", <value>);
            }
        }

entry.title: Re: Assign class/characteristics to equipment using migration cockpit
entry.content: I can add a new classification with this, but not update an existing one. What i have to do in this case?

entry.title: Re: Uncheking Batch Management
entry.content:
Hello @SorePG 

See the note 533383 - Resetting the batch management requirement 

SAP delivers a conversion tool to switch batch management off without clearing stocks and documents. The tool is subject to additional charge.

I used a similar tool to set batch management requirement. It's worth the money.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Worfklow is not triggered after saving PR via ME51N
entry.content: I did the following settings. But when I create in ME51N a PR no Workflow is triggered.

entry.title: Re: Adding Variables to Predictive Analysis
entry.content:
Hi,

Forecast In Chart always uses the data as displayed in the chart.

In my example since data is displayed at the day granularity, data is processed at the day granularity and forecast is provided at the day granularity. If I want to get a forecast for the whole month I must request a 30 periods forecast, I will get 30 predictions corresponding to 30 days:

(Date hierarchy level is set to "day")

If I want to work at a month level I just have to change the chart configuration and display the data aggregated by month. In that case data is processed at the month level, and 1 period forecast corresponds to a 1 months forecast (1 predictions for one month):

(Date hierarchy level is set to "month")

Choosing the right time granularity is first a matter of how you want to consume the forecast. If you are interested in consuming the predicted value at the month level, then it's better to forecast at the month level directly than forecasting at the day level and then summing the result (because you are also summing the prediction error). 

Regards.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Classical MRP vs MRP Live
entry.content:
Please find the of difference list in table format.

FeatureClassic MRP (t-codes MD01/MD02/MD03)MRP live (t-code MD01N)
MRP listMRP parameter "Create MRP list" can be used to control whether MRP list should be created.MRP writes that no MRP lists live as MRP lists were intended to check the MRP result. MRP lists were used to find materials with issues quickly. MRP lists are snapshots of the material supply and demand situation during the last MRP run. The snapshot is often outdated. With HANA, stock/requirements lists can be read at high speed. MRP cockpit can be used to determine materials with late supply issues in real time. T-code md_mrp_force_classic shows all master data-related issues(like no production version found). In SAP S/4HANA there is no need for outdated MRP lists.
Scope of planningMRP parameter "Scope of planning" can be used to combine a group of plants or MRP areas.Scope of planning is not supported. MRP Live can plan many materials in many plants. It always plans all MRP Areas.
creation indicatorsClassic MRP has a complicated set of rules for instructing the MRP run to create either purchase requisitions or planned orders for externally procured materials. MRP parameters "Create purchase req." and "Schedule lines" can be used.MRP live on HANA always creates purchase requisitions for externally procured materials and schedule lines if there is a valid scheduling agreement or source list with a scheduling agreement. MRP live on HANA always creates planned orders for inhouse production materials.
opening periodIn classic MRP, the parameter "Creation indicator for Purchase Requisitions" can be set as 2:Purchase requisitions in opening period.MRP live on HANA doesn't consider opening period.
Planning horizonMRP parameter "Processing key" NETPL can be set on ERP systems, but it is removed in S/4HANA systems.MRP live doesn't support planning horizon
processing keyMRP parameter "Processing key" NETPL can be set on ERP systems, but it is removed in S/4HANA systems.The indicator "Regenerative planning" in MRP live replaces the MRP parameter "Processing key". If it is set, it works similarly like the processing key = NEUPL in classic MRP.
schedulingOn ERP systems, if the MRP parameter "Scheduling" is set to "2 Lead Time Scheduling and Capacity Planning", Detailed planning, Rate-based planning, and Rough-Cut Planning are all supported. On S/4HANA systems only detailed scheduling is supported.On S/4HANA systems only detailed scheduling is supported. If Rate-based and rough-cut routings are maintained in the production version, exception message MD425 occurs "62: Scheduling: Master data inconsistent (P2)". MRP live also supports advanced planning for PP/DS.
planning modeClassic MRP t-codes have 3 options for planning mode: 1 Adapt planning data (normal mode) 2 Re-explode BOM and routing 3 Delete and recreate planning dataNo planning mode 2. Planning mode 1 works different from classic MRP. For planning mode 1, either 100% reuse or delete and re- create. AMDP BADI PPH_MRP_REUSE_BADI can be used to influence system behavior.
simulationT-code MD02 has 2 indicators "Simulation mode" and "Display results prior to saving" to simulate MRP run. T-code MD03 has an indicator "Display

results prior to saving".		MRP live doesn't have simulation mode.
BOM componentsT-code MD02 can be used to run MRP for single-item, multi-leve. There is also an indicator "Also plan unchanged components"MRP live has an indicator "BOM components" if components are also to be planned. Indicator "Also plan unchanged components" is added to MRP live by note 2584201. If a change number is involved in BOM changes, please check KBA 2611243 to make sure MRP live works correctly.
MPS PlanningT code MD40/MD41/MD42 /MD43 can be used to run MPS planning. These tcodes are obsolete in S/4 hana systems.Field Material scope is added in MRP live. If it is set to S or A MPS materials are planned.
Stock Transfer materialsClassic MRP can use the field scope of planning to plan both supplying plant and receiving plantIndicator stock transfer materials are added in MRP live to control whether you want to plan materials in the supplying plants during the planning run for stock transfer.
Performance logReport RMMDMONI and RMMDPERF can be used to analyze classic MRP performanceA new field Name for the performance log is added in MD01N. Performance logs can later be checked by tcode MD_MRP_PERFLOG. No Performance log per material.
MRP ControllerUser exit M61X0001 can be used to influence which materials are selected to be planned classic MRP run.Fields product group and MRP controller are added in MRP live.
User exit keyUser exit M61X0001 can be used influence which materials are selected to be planned during classic MRP runUser exit M61X0001 is not supported by tcode MD01N. The BADI MRP_DISPATCHER_BADI can be used to implement own logic

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hello Olga,

I hope you're doing well. First, I want to thank you for your efforts in clarifying the Custom Code Migration app—it was very helpful and clear to me. However, I have a question regarding the complexity of custom code in the graphical analysis.

You mentioned that we should remove it from our scope, but I’m not sure why. My understanding is that if something is complex and changes frequently, we should analyze it using the graphics in the analysis and determine how to handle it. So why should it be excluded from the scope? Could you clarify whether it refers to SAP standard code or something else?

Thank you for your time!

entry.title: Re: Exploring the RSS feeds of the new SAP Community platform
entry.content: @mamartins Thanks for sharing... I can see that it is possible to access the URL if you open it in a browser but I'm experiencing a similar behaviour when trying to get the out of the box RSS from a reader.

entry.title: Re: 中信保在SAP云版本的应用方案
entry.content:
您好，

SAP S/4HANA Cloud 中的 SAP Credit Management 可用于通过出口信用保险处理外贸业务中提到的场景。操作方法如下：

1.**信用额度管理：** SAP Credit Management 允许您管理每个业务伙伴的信用额度。您可以定义和监控客户的特定信用额度，例如中信公司提供的保险信用额度。此功能使您能够管理和跟踪这些信用额度的使用情况。

2.**信用敞口报表：** 系统自动报告业务伙伴的承诺，例如发货和所有未清应收账款。这有助于根据定义的额度跟踪信用敞口。

3.**处理超额限额：** 可配置 SAP Credit Management 以处理信用利用率超过设置额度的场景。可以设置超限补贴，在这种情况下，保险额度为 10,000，总允许限额可设置为 10,500。

4.**冻结客户发货：** 如果客户的信用利用率超过额度，或未清应收账款逾期，SAP Credit Management 可以自动冻结客户的其他交易或发货，从而有助于有效管理有风险的应收账款。

5.**保险申请和调整：** 创建销售订单时，更新信用利用率，收到付款后，将自动调整此利用率。这反映了信用敞口和利用率的实时更新。

6.**集成外部信用机构：** SAP Credit Management 允许通过 XML 接口与外部信用机构（例如中信）集成，从而能够灵活地将外部信用评估和保险信息纳入信用管理流程。

通过利用 SAP Credit Management 的这些功能，您可以管理出口信用保险，包括超额信用额度，并处理超额限额和逾期情况。

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Spoiler
Hi @chaimae_0709 

Hi Chaimae,

exactly: first analyze how to handle it. If the complex custom code can replaced by already available in S/4HANA SAP standard, or if it is outdated or old business logic, or can be re-implemented on S/4HANA in other way  - remove from scope. Otherwise you would need first invest efforts to adapt this complex code for S/4HANA in order to remove it afterwards anyway.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Spool server is having an error when it is requested multiple pages for an adobe form
entry.content:
Good morning gurus, 

I have a requirement to generate a spool with an adobe form with multiple same pages. The form contains 1 page and the number of pages that are going to be created is specified in nast-anzal field. In my printing program I set the following fields to allow the spool creation:

gs_outputparams-dest = nast-ldest.
gs_outputparams-reqnew = abap_true.
gs_outputparams-covtitle = nast-objky.
gs_outputparams-reqimm = abap_true.

I first call FP_JOB_OPEN (which receives the mentioned fields) and after that I iterate (based on the anzal field) the call to the form function. When testing with the device LP01 (with device type PDFUC) it was working as expected, several same pages in one form. But when changed to the required output device (with device type ricoh), it works fine when generating 1 page, but when it is required more pages, it fails and the spool is completed with 0 pages. I have checked and it fails in the form function in the 3rd iteration with status 2 (system error). I guess it is happening because of the device type but I cannot change it so, does anyone know how to prepare the form so the device accepts it or another way to print multiple pages without iteration?

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing 24.11 Release new capabilities
entry.content: Hello @jborrerom , for serialized materials (with a Serial Number Profile), Next Number was not used for SFC ID generation at all, MATERIAL-SERIALNUMBER pattern was hardcoded instead. Moving forward, you can leverage Naming Patterns for Serialized SFCs to define your own patterns for serialized materials. Hope it clarifies, Manoel

entry.title: Re: How to trigger RAP Event in Unmanaged Scenario ?
entry.content: Yes, the event should be raised in Save Method in case of unmanaged scenario.

entry.title: Content 'Not mobile accessible' for non-mobiles (Tablets and Dex)
entry.content:
We received 2 notifications from users who are not able to acces content on Samsung tablets. When starting an e-learning they see the message "not mobile accessible". We think it might be browser related, switching from Samsung Internet to another worked.
It is correct the content is not made available for mobile use in our LMS. But we never had this issue with tablets.

On top of this we're currently testing Samsung Dex functionality. With this functionality users connect their mobile to a screen with mouse and keyboard. 
While using this set-up, the browser does not switch to desktopmode. In this case, when starting content we also see the message "not mobile accessible". (Once we switch to desktopmode the issue is fixed)

We're looking for a solution to acces content while using the Dex set-up without having to instruct users to switch to desktopmode.

Could someone tell me which parameters are used by SAP to determine whether a user is "mobile" and is denied to acces content?
Is it the OS Name, Device Type, User Agent, resolution size or something else?

entry.title: Re: Partial Quantity Billing for Billing Plans
entry.content:
Hello,

You can try the following:

1. **Use Delivery-Related Billing**: Ensure that the item category in your sales order is set up for delivery-related billing. This will allow the invoice to reference the delivery document, which reflects the actual delivered quantity.

2. **Change the Quantity in the Billing Due List**:
- Access the `Create Billing Documents` app.
- During the invoice creation process, from the billing due list, you can manually adjust the quantity to match the delivered quantity (in this case, 8).
- Select the delivery document corresponding to the actual delivered quantity.

3. **Ensure Billing Plan Adjustments**:
- If your billing plan defaults to the entire sales order quantity, ensure the plan can accommodate changes. This involves checking the configuration of your billing plan to see if it allows for quantity adjustments.
- Often, customizing is required at the item category level to allow changes to be reflected in billing documents.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How to correct Data in ODQMON
entry.content:
Hi Kiran

 

Data in ODQMON is not changeable. The data can be adjusted either via a customer exit (2684463 FAQ: User-Exit/BAdI in data loads from ODP SAPI source systems).

Another option is to write a formula or routine in the Transformation on BW side to adjust the value.

 

br

Roland

entry.title: DMS opening a file
entry.content:
Hello! Tell me please, When uploading a file to cv01n

 

The EXCEL file open, returns to the home page and returns an error that such a document exists. We don't need it, we want to stay on the same screen.

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi Olga,

Thank you for your response and the detailed explanation. Could you explain how complexity is defined in this context?

entry.title: Re: Issue with Intercompany Royalties Settlement - 55I
entry.content:
Hello,

The error you're encountering seems to be related to the inability to determine an input tax code for a line item during the pricing procedure. Here are some steps you can take to resolve this:

1. **Check Tax Code in Pricing Procedure:**
- Ensure that the tax code is properly defined in your pricing procedure. Verify the condition records for taxes are correctly maintained.

2. **Tax Code Determination:**
- Navigate to your configuration environment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and check the tax code determination settings. You can use the search function to open activities related to tax code assignments.

3. **Review Master Data:**
- Double-check your master data to ensure all necessary data for tax calculations is available and correct. This includes ensuring that all relevant fields are filled in with the correct information.

4. **Examine Message and Diagnosis:**
- The message no. WS533 suggests a problem in determining the input tax code during pricing. Review the detailed error message and consider running a pricing analysis to see where the determination process might be failing.

5. **Condition Records:**
- Go through your condition records to ensure all necessary tax condition types have valid entries.

6. **Create/Update Tax Conditions:**
- If necessary, create new tax condition entries or update existing ones to ensure they align with the current master data and business requirements.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: I need to avoid ME053 for pur. req. for certain type...
entry.content:
Hello @Ilfan 

The message ME053 is "Status "&" of material & does not allow external procurement". If a material status doesn't allow external procurement, it is not allowed irrespective of purchasing document types.

You can't implement your requirement in standard.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP Warehouse Operator – A New iPhone Application for the Warehouse
entry.content: Hello @ruiyang_shen, we are currently looking into making the app also available in China. I hope to have an update for you soon.

entry.title: Cannot Download AdobeForm Template from SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Hello,

One month ago everything was fine with our SAP Business ByDesing AdobeForm Templates.

Today I tried to download any template and it does not work. Once per every Web Browser (Chrome, Edge) I could download a ZIP file which proved to be empty. After that, no file is downloaded, not even after cleaning cache and cookies or closing browser. The green message "Template downloaded" shows, but there is no file.

Upload, however, functions.

Any similar experiences?

Thanks in advance for your help.

KR,

Igor Barbaric

entry.title: Re: Electronic Invoicing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi @chaimae_0709 ,

the Custom Code Migration app calculates the complexity based on the Halstead complexity measures. For packages, the Halstead difficulty is aggregated using an H-index algorithm.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: I need to avoid ME053 for pur. req. for certain type...
entry.content:
Hello @DominikTylczyn

But if I need this to be done anyway, what can I do?

entry.title: How to configure a communication channel as an outbound email channel?
entry.content: I would like to configure an email as an outbound channel for cases. Can I configure an email just an outbound email for cases?

entry.title: How to Edit Multiple Selected Items Individually in a List Report Using an Action Button?
entry.content:
I have a List Report in my SAP Fiori Elements application, and I want to allow the user to select multiple lines and then click on an action button to edit each line individually.

Currently, my action button accepts a parameter where I can pass a single value. However, this value is static, meaning it applies the same value to all selected items, which doesn't allow flexibility for individual changes.

What I'm trying to achieve is the ability to dynamically pass different values (e.g., a unique part number) for each selected row when the button is clicked. I would like to know how I can flexibly manage dialog parameters for each selected item, or if there's a better approach to achieve this functionality in the context of a List Report with SAP RAP.

Has anyone implemented something similar or can suggest a solution for this scenario?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: how to implement to method IF_EX_FAGL_COND_ACC_ASSIGN...
entry.content:
This is the Example class of a BAdI, what are you doing there 😉

  • Use a where-used search, you should find the relevant BAdI using this interface
    (FAGL_COND_ACC_ASSIGNMENT) 
  • Implement this BAdI, add your code to  your implementing class and activate the class and implementation.

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content: Thank you @OlgaDolinskaja .

entry.title: Re: Usage-based subscription with minimum fixed consumpti...
entry.content:
Hi Carsten,

Thank you for your suggestions!
The Volume price condition with two tiers seems a valid solution but the suitable quantity really seems to correspond to the scenario I'm trying to achieve.
I tried to set it up but I might be missing something as I'm not getting the expected result..
I created a new pricing field called quantity1 (type = quantity, source = external) -> in the price element specification I added the component "suitable quantity" + the path to find and calculate the price as shown in the screenshot -> I added the price element specification to the pricing scheme -> in business configuration I mapped field quantity1 to attribute "Product Configuration" for subscription item which I called "Minimum usage".
Finally I created a subscription with a configurable product, I added attribute "Minimum usage" with quantity 10 EA -> I created a usage record of 3 EA for the technical resource of the subscription I just created -> the expected result would be an open bill with quantity 3EA and price for the minimum usage of 10EA vs result I got: the bill is not created at all.

Maybe you have an idea of any missing configuration in my process?

Thank you very much in advance.
Kr,
Elena

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 Released – Safeguarding Customer Investments and Maximizing Value
entry.content:
Hello @stefan_baeuerle,

still no support for AMD Epyc CPUs?

Is this planned for SPS09 or a future SPS?

 

Martin
